title: Cissus muricata
intro: Cissus muricata, also known as Vitex trifolia var. muricata or grape ivy, is a woody climber native to tropical Asia, characterized by its purple-colored flowers and edible fruit. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cissus muricata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Cissus muricata is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or extracted into a tincture or syrup, to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of fever and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Cissus muricata is classified in the family Vitaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the grape or vine family. This plant is native to tropical Africa and is also known as the "Wild Grape" or "Climbing Grape". The classification of Cissus muricata is further broken down into the genus Cissus and the species Cissus muricata. Cissus is a genus of flowering plants that are characterized by their woody vines and edible fruits. The Vitaceae family, which includes Cissus muricata, is one of the 64 families within the order Rosales.
uses: Cissus muricata is used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and stems are used in herbal remedies to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, gout, and other joint-related problems. Cissus muricata is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating infections and wounds. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce pain and inflammation in the body. parts: Cissus muricata, commonly known as the rock climbing grape or grape ivy, is a plant with various medicinal properties. The leaves and stems of the plant are used in traditional medicine, particularly in African and Asian cultures. The leaves are said to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making them useful for treating various health conditions. side_effects: Cissus muricata, commonly known as the rock vine or kudungu, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
